note: Live tweet embedding/API fetching depends on external Twitter/X credentials and services, so all cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing the media source plugin id, source field types, metadata attributes, the formatter, config keys, and the fetcher service.

evals.prompt: In Drupal, which core Media source plugin id does the Media Entity Twitter module provide, and what core module does it depend on?
evals.expected_output: It provides a MediaSource plugin with id `twitter` (label "Twitter") and depends on the core `media` module.

evals.prompt: When I create a Media type using the Twitter source in Drupal, which source field types are allowed and what label does the auto-created source field get?
evals.expected_output: Allowed field types are string, string_long, and link; the auto-created source field is labeled "Tweet URL".

evals.prompt: Without configuring any Twitter API credentials, which metadata fields can Media Entity Twitter extract from a tweet URL?
evals.expected_output: Only `id` (Tweet ID) and `user` (the author handle) are extracted from the URL via regex; richer fields require enabling the Twitter API.

evals.prompt: After enabling the Twitter API option in Media Entity Twitter, name some of the extra metadata attributes the source exposes for mapping to fields.
evals.expected_output: With use_twitter_api enabled: image, image_local, image_local_uri, content, retweet_count, profile_image_url_https, created_time, and user_name.

evals.prompt: Which field formatter does Media Entity Twitter provide to render an embedded tweet, and what setting controls whether the parent/previous tweet is shown?
evals.expected_output: The `twitter_embed` formatter; its boolean `conversation` setting (default off) toggles showing the previous tweet — when off it sets data-conversation=none.

evals.prompt: Which Drupal config object and key set the directory where Media Entity Twitter stores generated tweet thumbnails, and what is the default value?
In Drupal code, which Media Entity Twitter service fetches tweet data from the API, and what must you call before fetchTweet()?
evals.expected_output: The `media_entity_twitter.tweet_fetcher` service (TweetFetcher); call setCredentials(consumer_key, consumer_secret, oauth_access_token, oauth_access_token_secret) before fetchTweet($id), which caches responses in the `tweets` bin for 90 days.

evals.prompt: Does Media Entity Twitter's URL matching accept x.com URLs in addition to twitter.com, and which providers does the source report?
evals.expected_output: Yes — the validation regex matches both twitter.com and x.com status/statuses URLs, and getProviders() returns ['Twitter', 'X'].
